notes: Third validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school is committed to academic excellence, nurturing personal relationships, and inspiring a growing passion for creativity and learning.
The school's core values include academic excellence, relationships, focusing on the whole child, community service, accountability, and health and wellness.
Its vision says the school uses innovative methods to provide quality education in a smaller, more responsive learning environment.
Official materials say its ENRICH program includes topics like public speaking, emotional intelligence, personal finance, teamwork, leadership, and healthy living.
The school says it applies brain-based research methods and hands-on learning in a brain-compatible environment.
Stephen Gay is listed on the administrative team as Executive Director.
Its mission emphasizes academic achievement, creativity, technological sophistication, a love of learning, accountability, self-esteem, and the development of good citizens.
The school says exceptional instruction by highly skilled, qualified, and devoted teachers should produce the highest possible student outcomes.
Official history language says the school was founded in 1998 as one of North Carolina's 100 charter schools.
Its website describes the school as providing a civil environment for academic excellence.
The board page says the school is not affiliated with any other charter school or management organization.
Robert L. Luddy is listed as chairman of the board.
Its official branding includes the phrase "Blazing Trails in Education."
The school says it transforms lives through authentic, collaborative learning experiences.
Its Pioneer Core Values include curiosity, resilience, and leadership.
Academic pages highlight both the Core Knowledge Sequence and Singapore Math.
The technology program includes a student technology help desk and stylus-enabled Chromebooks.
Erin Fisher is listed as board chair.
Its homepage tagline says, "Build confidence for lifelong learning."
Another official phrase on the site is "Engage. Experience. Achieve."
The school says it cultivates critical thinking and a lifelong love of learning through rigorous academics, experiential education, and core values.
Those published core values are Integrity, Respect, Compassion, Responsibility, Wisdom, and Leadership.
Its founding story says the school opened in fall 2015 and initially partnered with Team CFA to use the Core Knowledge Sequence.
Its board meets on the fourth Wednesday of the month at 6:30 pm unless otherwise posted.
Its official slogan is "Challenge. Character. Community."
Its mission says the school is dedicated to educating the whole child through challenging instruction, strong character development, and collaborative family and community involvement.
The vision describes a pathway by which children mature into confident young adults ready to contribute to local and global communities.
Official history language says it is a National School of Character and the first and longest operating charter school in Union County.
Its stated values revolve around high academic expectations, high character expectations, school community, and service to others.
Amy Yermack is listed as Interim Head of School, and the school notes she has spent 14 years there in multiple leadership roles.
